ackels2021.authors: Ackels, T., Erskine, A., Dasgupta, D., Marin, A. C., Warner, T. P. A., Tootoonian, S., Fukunaga, I., Harris, J. J. & Schaefer, A. T.
ackels2021.title: Fast odour dynamics are encoded in the olfactory system and guide behaviour
ackels2021.note: Mice discriminate odour correlations up to 40 Hz — far faster than single-ORN kinetics allow.

angelaki2004.authors: Angelaki, D. E., Shaikh, A. G., Green, A. M. & Dickman, J. D.
angelaki2004.title: Neurons compute internal models of the physical laws of motion
angelaki2004.note: Brainstem and cerebellar neurons resolve the tilt–translation ambiguity — neurons implementing a specified estimator.

atick1992.authors: Atick, J. J. & Redlich, A. N.
atick1992.title: What does the retina know about natural scenes?
atick1992.note: The SNR-dependent whitening solution: bandpass at high SNR, lowpass at low SNR.

attneave1954.title: Some informational aspects of visual perception
attneave1954.note: The founding statement that perception exploits redundancy in the world.

barlow1961.title: Possible principles underlying the transformation of sensory messages
barlow1961.venue: In: Rosenblith, W. A. (ed.), Sensory Communication, MIT Press, pp. 217–234
barlow1961.note: Redundancy reduction as the organising principle of sensory coding.

baylor1979.authors: Baylor, D. A., Lamb, T. D. & Yau, K.-W.
baylor1979.title: Responses of retinal rods to single photons
baylor1979.note: The rod detects a single photon — the sensor is already at the physical limit.

bhandawat2007.authors: Bhandawat, V., Olsen, S. R., Gouwens, N. W., Schlief, M. L. & Wilson, R. I.
bhandawat2007.title: Sensory processing in the Drosophila antennal lobe increases reliability and separability of ensemble odor representations
bhandawat2007.note: Weak ORN input is amplified, strong input is not — broadening tuning and equalising distances between odours.

bolding2018.authors: Bolding, K. A. & Franks, K. M.
bolding2018.title: Recurrent cortical circuits implement concentration-invariant odor coding
bolding2018.note: Piriform recurrence, not bulbar input, produces concentration invariance.

bosco2001.title: Proprioception from a spinocerebellar perspective
bosco2001.note: The source of the global-limb-variable claim. Note the review is explicit that the global representation originates partly in the biomechanical linkages of the limb, not in neural processing alone.

bosco2003.title: Modulation of dorsal spinocerebellar responses to limb movement. II. Effect of sensory input
bosco2003.note: Decouples limb geometry from endpoint position to ask whether the global representation is neural or biomechanical. About half of 89 cells changed response pattern; muscle stimulation revealed explicit representation of stimulus parameters. The answer is partly neural, not wholly.

buchsbaum1983.title: Trichromacy, opponent colours coding and optimum colour information transmission in the retina
buchsbaum1983.venue: Proceedings of the Royal Society B 220(1218):89–113
buchsbaum1983.note: Opponent axes derived from first principles — the closest existing analogue to a derived coordinate system for odour.

buckaxel1991.title: A novel multigene family may encode odorant receptors: a molecular basis for odor recognition
buckaxel1991.note: Discovery of the odorant receptor gene family.

caron2013.authors: Caron, S. J. C., Ruta, V., Abbott, L. F. & Axel, R.
caron2013.title: Random convergence of olfactory inputs in the Drosophila mushroom body
caron2013.note: Kenyon cells sample glomeruli at random — no discernible structure with respect to odour tuning.

carr1990.title: A circuit for detection of interaural time differences in the brain stem of the barn owl
carr1990.note: Delay lines and coincidence detectors, as Jeffress proposed — the mechanism exists, in the owl.

celani2014.authors: Celani, A., Villermaux, E. & Vergassola, M.
celani2014.title: Odor landscapes in turbulent environments
celani2014.note: The statistical structure of turbulent odour plumes: intermittency and whiff/blank distributions.

chandrashekar2006.authors: Chandrashekar, J., Hoon, M. A., Ryba, N. J. P. & Zuker, C. S.
chandrashekar2006.title: The receptors and cells for mammalian taste
chandrashekar2006.note: The labelled-line case: distinct cell types with unique receptors, each tuned to one basic taste.

cullen2012.title: The vestibular system: multimodal integration and encoding of self-motion for motor control
cullen2012.note: Vestibular nuclei cancel self-generated head motion but not passive motion — explicit subtraction of an efference-copy prediction.

dan1996.authors: Dan, Y., Atick, J. J. & Reid, R. C.
dan1996.title: Efficient coding of natural scenes in the lateral geniculate nucleus: experimental test of a computational theory
dan1996.note: LGN responses to natural movies are temporally white, as whitening predicts.

dean2005.authors: Dean, I., Harper, N. S. & McAlpine, D.
dean2005.title: Neural population coding of sound level adapts to stimulus statistics
dean2005.note: Midbrain neurons re-tune their dynamic range to the current sound-level distribution — dynamic efficient coding, measured.

fetsch2011.authors: Fetsch, C. R., Pouget, A., DeAngelis, G. C. & Angelaki, D. E.
fetsch2011.title: Neural correlates of reliability-based cue weighting during multisensory integration
fetsch2011.note: The direct neural test. MSTd population decoding predicted behavioural cue weighting including its modest deviations from optimality — so the animals are near-optimal rather than optimal, and the neurons track the shortfall.

field1987.title: Relations between the statistics of natural images and the response properties of cortical cells
field1987.venue: Journal of the Optical Society of America A 4(12):2379–2394
field1987.note: The 1/f amplitude spectrum and the argument that cortical filters are matched to it.

field2005.authors: Field, G. D., Sampath, A. P. & Rieke, F.
field2005.title: Retinal processing near absolute threshold: from behavior to mechanism
field2005.note: What single-photon sensitivity does and does not imply about noiseless transmission.

friedrich2001.title: Dynamic optimization of odor representations by slow temporal patterning of mitral cell activity
friedrich2001.note: Decorrelation over time in zebrafish bulb: ensembles separate without individual tuning sharpening.

ganguli2014.title: Efficient sensory encoding and Bayesian inference with heterogeneous neural populations
ganguli2014.note: Optimal tuning-curve density is proportional to the prior — the machinery for comparing receptor arrays across modalities.

gracheva2010.authors: Gracheva, E. O., Ingolia, N. T., Kelly, Y. M., Cordero-Morales, J. F., Hollopeter, G., Chesler, A. T., Sánchez, E. E., Perez, J. C., Weissman, J. S. & Julius, D.
gracheva2010.title: Molecular basis of infrared detection by snakes
gracheva2010.note: TRPA1 as the infrared transducer — a nociceptive channel repurposed as an imaging sensor.

haberly2001.title: Parallel-distributed processing in olfactory cortex: new insights from morphological and physiological analysis of neuronal circuitry
haberly2001.note: Piriform as association cortex rather than a primary sensory area.

johnson2001.title: The roles and functions of cutaneous mechanoreceptors
johnson2001.note: Each of the four afferent classes serves a distinct perceptual function, and each receptor is specialised for it.

kandel2021.authors: Kandel, E. R., Koester, J. D., Mack, S. H. & Siegelbaum, S. A. (eds)
kandel2021.title: Principles of Neural Science, 6th edition — Part IV: Perception
kandel2021.venue: McGraw Hill. ISBN 978-1259642241. Part IV edited by Albright, T. D. & Bruno, R. M.
kandel2021.note: The standard textbook, used here to check and fill the course rather than to source arguments. Where a claim in this course rests on Kandel, the citing prose names the chapter. Chapters 17-29 cover sensory coding, somatosensation, touch, pain, vision, audition, vestibular, and the chemical senses.

koulakov2011.title: Sparse incomplete representations: a potential role of olfactory granule cells
koulakov2011.note: Sparse bulbar codes from a balance of excitation and granule-cell inhibition.

krishnamurthy2022.authors: Krishnamurthy, K., Hermundstad, A. M., Mora, T., Walczak, A. M. & Balasubramanian, V.
krishnamurthy2022.title: Disorder and the neural representation of complex odors
krishnamurthy2022.note: Argues disorder is functional: introducing any order into receptor binding degrades similarity preservation.

laughlin1981.title: A simple coding procedure enhances a neuron's information capacity
laughlin1981.note: The founding empirical success: fly LMC contrast-response matches the cumulative distribution of natural contrasts.

lewicki2002.title: Efficient coding of natural sounds
lewicki2002.note: Filters optimised to encode natural sounds reproduce auditory-nerve tuning — audition's answer to the derivation vision got for colour.

litwinkumar2017.authors: Litwin-Kumar, A., Harris, K. D., Axel, R., Sompolinsky, H. & Abbott, L. F.
litwinkumar2017.title: Optimal degrees of synaptic connectivity
litwinkumar2017.note: Representation dimension is maximised at the connection degree actually observed in mushroom body and cerebellum.

malnic1999.authors: Malnic, B., Hirono, J., Sato, T. & Buck, L. B.
malnic1999.title: Combinatorial receptor codes for odors
malnic1999.note: One receptor recognises many odorants and one odorant many receptors — the combinatorial code.

mcalpine2001.authors: McAlpine, D., Jiang, D. & Palmer, A. R.
mcalpine2001.title: A neural code for low-frequency sound localization in mammals
mcalpine2001.note: Two broad hemispheric channels rather than a Jeffress place map — optimal given the ITD range a small head actually experiences.

mcdermott2011.authors: McDermott, J. H. & Simoncelli, E. P.
mcdermott2011.title: Sound texture perception via statistics of the auditory periphery: evidence from sound synthesis
mcdermott2011.note: Time-averaged statistics of an auditory model are sufficient to synthesise recognisable textures — the ensemble, characterised.

olsen2010.authors: Olsen, S. R., Bhandawat, V. & Wilson, R. I.
olsen2010.title: Divisive normalization in olfactory population codes
olsen2010.note: Lateral inhibition in the antennal lobe scales with total ORN activity — global normalisation, not structured decorrelation.

olshausen1996.authors: Olshausen, B. A. & Field, D. J.
olshausen1996.title: Emergence of simple-cell receptive field properties by learning a sparse code for natural images
olshausen1996.note: Sparseness alone yields localised, oriented, bandpass receptive fields.

pitkow2012.title: Decorrelation and efficient coding by retinal ganglion cells
pitkow2012.note: The honest counterweight: most retinal decorrelation comes from nonlinearity, not from the receptive field.

ruderman1994.title: Statistics of natural images: scaling in the woods
ruderman1994.note: Scale invariance measured in the woods; the empirical basis for treating natural-image statistics as scale-free.

ruderman1998.authors: Ruderman, D. L., Cronin, T. W. & Chiao, C.-C.
ruderman1998.title: Statistics of cone responses to natural images: implications for visual coding
ruderman1998.venue: Journal of the Optical Society of America A 15(8):2036–2045
ruderman1998.note: PCA of natural LMS cone signals recovers the opponent axes empirically.

saal2014.authors: Saal, H. P. & Bensmaia, S. J.
saal2014.title: Touch is a team effort: interplay of submodalities in cutaneous sensibility
saal2014.note: The corrective to labelled lines in touch: natural stimuli excite all afferent classes and cortical neurons pool across them.

shen2013.authors: Shen, K., Tootoonian, S. & Laurent, G.
shen2013.title: Encoding of mixtures in a simple olfactory system
shen2013.note: Strong hypoadditivity in locust PNs; sparse Kenyon cells often signal single components within mixtures.

shera2002.authors: Shera, C. A., Guinan, J. J. & Oxenham, A. J.
shera2002.title: Revised estimates of human cochlear tuning from otoacoustic and behavioral measurements
shera2002.note: Human cochlear tuning is sharper than the other mammals measured — a species difference a normative theory should explain.

simoncelli2001.authors: Simoncelli, E. P. & Olshausen, B. A.
simoncelli2001.title: Natural image statistics and neural representation
simoncelli2001.note: The standard synthesis of the efficient-coding programme in vision.

smith2006.authors: Smith, E. C. & Lewicki, M. S.
smith2006.title: Efficient auditory coding
smith2006.note: A spike-based code optimised for natural sounds matches cochlear filter estimates quantitatively.

srinivasan1982.authors: Srinivasan, M. V., Laughlin, S. B. & Dubs, A.
srinivasan1982.title: Predictive coding: a fresh view of inhibition in the retina
srinivasan1982.venue: Proceedings of the Royal Society B 216(1205):427–459
srinivasan1982.note: Centre–surround as subtraction of a statistical prediction; predicts the surround weakens at low light.

tootoonian2022.authors: Tootoonian, S., Schaefer, A. T. & Latham, P. E.
tootoonian2022.title: Sparse connectivity for MAP inference in linear models using sister mitral cells
tootoonian2022.note: MAP inference without all-to-all connectivity, using the sister-cell architecture of the bulb.

tootoonian2025.authors: Tootoonian, S., True, A. C., Stark, E., Crimaldi, J. P. & Schaefer, A. T.
tootoonian2025.title: Quantifying spectral information about source separation in multisource odour plumes
tootoonian2025.note: High-frequency components of plume correlations are most informative about source separation when sources are close.

uchida2003.title: Speed and accuracy of olfactory discrimination in the rat
uchida2003.note: One sniff, under 200 ms, at maximum accuracy — the deadline the olfactory code must meet.

vanhateren1998.authors: van Hateren, J. H. & van der Schaaf, A.
vanhateren1998.title: Independent component filters of natural images compared with simple cells in primary visual cortex
vanhateren1998.venue: Proceedings of the Royal Society B 265(1394):359–366
vanhateren1998.note: The natural-image database behind much of this literature, plus the ICA-to-simple-cell comparison.

vergassola2007.authors: Vergassola, M., Villermaux, E. & Shraiman, B. I.
vergassola2007.title: 'Infotaxis' as a strategy for searching without gradients
vergassola2007.note: Search by maximising expected information gain; reproduces casting and zigzagging.

wachowiak2011.title: All in a sniff: olfaction as a model for active sensing
wachowiak2011.note: Sniffing as the active-sensing act that structures all olfactory input.

wolpert1995.authors: Wolpert, D. M., Ghahramani, Z. & Jordan, M. I.
wolpert1995.title: An internal model for sensorimotor integration
wolpert1995.note: Direct evidence for a forward model: error propagation follows optimal state estimation.

zhou2018.authors: Zhou, Y., Smith, B. H. & Sharpee, T. O.
zhou2018.title: Hyperbolic geometry of the olfactory space
zhou2018.note: Odours map to a three-dimensional hyperbolic space derived from natural co-occurrence statistics.
